title: Outdoor Firewood Storage Rack
summary: Complete plans to a medium sized outdoor firewood storage rack.
description: Complete plans to a medium sized outdoor firewood storage rack. The build is complete with materials list, tool list, cut list as well as assembly instructions.

steps.title: Cutting the Floor and the Roof
steps.body: Using the Kreg AdapMve Cu[ng System Plunge-Cut Track Saw Cut your plywood into One 4’ x 18" piece and One 4’ x 22” piece

steps.title: Cutting the Posts
steps.body: -Cut two 2x4’s into 40” pieces. One end must be cut at 7-degrees angle. -Cut two 2x4’s into 38 ¼” pieces. One end must be cut at a 7-degrees angle.

steps.title: Cutting and attaching the Feet
steps.body: Cut five 2x4’s at 3” lengths for feet and attach to the four corners and one in the middle. Use wood glue and screws.

steps.title: Attaching the posts
steps.body: Following the direcMons on the Kreg Pocket-hole Jig 720PRO (Or other Kreg Pocket-hole jigs), drill pocket holes for screws on the ends of each 2x4 post. Attach to base with the longer pieces as rear posts and the 7-degree angled pieces forward.

steps.title: Inserting supports and attaching roof
steps.body: Using pocket hole screws, a5ach the roof with the overhang to the front. Using pocket hole screws, insert support 2x4’s in the sides and at the rear. Install at 3” height and at 15” height

steps.title: Attaching Roof
steps.body: Opional prior to adding roof: -Spray/stain or paint structure depending on outdoor climates in your area. Attach roofing material of your choice according to the instructions. I used roofing shingles to ensure that water drips away from the wood.
